2026-04-17T03:50:46.3035930Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3036732Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3037183Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3037562Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3037874Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3038115Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3038275Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3038458Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3038631Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3038867Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3039072Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3039360Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:50:46.3039623Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. 2026-04-17T03:53:29.2489794Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T03:51:51.483892821Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58258,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document chat isolation, observability, and updated error behaviors in Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 (commit a43d15f) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added several significant features and behavior changes to the AgentServer Responses library:\\n\\n1. **Chat isolation key enforcement** — A new security feature using the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header. When a response is created with this key, all subsequent GET/Cancel/DELETE/InputItems calls must supply the same key; mismatches return 404 to prevent cross-chat data leakage.\\n2. **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`** — A new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs all outbound Foundry storage API calls with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n3. **HttpClient instrumentation** — `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()` is now registered for outbound trace correlation in the OTLP path, exporting outbound HTTP client spans for full distributed traces through the Foundry storage layer.\\n4. **Updated error shapes** — Error responses now align with the container specification: cancel-after-terminal returns `invalid_request_error`, delete-not-found returns 404 (was 400), cancel-after-delete returns 404 (was 400).\\n5. **Metadata constraint validation** — Metadata maps are now validated: ≤16 key-value pairs, keys ≤64 characters, values ≤512 characters.\\n6. **Response ID validation** — Malformed response IDs in the path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are rejected with 400.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key is entirely undocumented** in the README. There is no Key Concepts section or example explaining the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, when to use it, how it enforces isolation across all endpoints, and what error callers receive on key mismatch. This is a security-relevant tenant-isolation feature that hosting developers need to understand.\\n- **Logging/Observability section is stale** — The README Logging section only mentions \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\u0026#34; It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or that outbound HttpClient instrumentation is now registered automatically. Developers troubleshooting Foundry connectivity issues won\\u0026#39;t know these logs exist or how to enable/read them.\\n- **Troubleshooting section has outdated cancel-error description** — The entry \\u0026#34;**400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state.\\u0026#34; is stale. Cancel-after-terminal now returns `invalid_request_error` (still 400 but with a specific error shape), and cancel-after-delete now returns 404. The troubleshooting section does not reflect these corrections.\\n- **Metadata constraints not documented** — The README does not mention that `metadata` maps have enforced limits (16 keys, 64-char keys, 512-char values). Developers who exceed these limits will see unexpected 400 errors with no guidance.\\n- **Response ID format requirements not documented** — No mention that malformed `previous_response_id` or path-parameter response IDs are rejected with 400.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n**1a. Add a \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection to Key Concepts** (insert after the \\u0026#34;Thread safety\\u0026#34; subsection, before `## Examples`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nTo prevent cross-chat data leakage in multi-tenant deployments, the server supports an optional\\n`x-agent-chat-isolation-key` request header. When a response is created with this header, every\\nsubsequent API call against that response (GET, Cancel, DELETE, InputItems) must supply the same\\nkey. Requests with a mismatched or absent key receive a standard **404 Not Found** response,\\nindistinguishable from a response that does not exist.\\n\\nIsolation is enforced both in-flight (before the response is persisted) and for stored responses.\\nNo enforcement is applied when the response was created without an isolation key.\\n\\nThe key is available to your handler via `context.Isolation` for any further application-level\\nisolation logic.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand the Logging subsection** (replace the current single-line paragraph under `### Logging` with):\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nOutbound HTTP calls to the Foundry storage layer are captured by `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`,\\nwhich logs every request and response at **Information** level (success) or **Warning/Error** level\\n(HTTP errors / exceptions), including:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and duration\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`\\n\\nWhen using the OTLP exporter path (`UseOtlpExporter` / non-Azure Monitor), outbound `HttpClient`\\nspans are also exported automatically via `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()`, enabling full\\ndistributed traces through the Foundry storage layer.\\n\\nConfigure minimum log levels in `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Debug\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Update the Troubleshooting \\u0026#34;Common errors\\u0026#34; section** (replace the current bulleted list):\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, `input` items are well-formed, metadata maps contain ≤16\\n  entries with keys ≤64 characters and values ≤512 characters, and that `previous_response_id`\\n  (when provided) is a valid response ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The path-parameter response ID or `previous_response_id`\\n  body field does not match the expected format. Use a valid response ID returned by the server.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`,\\n  `failed`, or `cancelled`). Check the current response status before cancelling.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL,\\n  has been deleted, or was accessed with a mismatched `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background\\n  response is still in-flight. Cancel-after-delete also returns 404.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed code snippets are required for these changes — the additions are conceptual\\nexplanations and updated bullet lists, not code examples. If a sample illustrating chat isolation\\nkey usage is desired, create a new sample file:\\n\\n**New file (optional):** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleChatIsolation.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Sample: Using chat isolation key for multi-tenant deployments\\n// This sample shows how to read the isolation key from ResponseContext.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README already contains all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs Chat isolation subsection added**\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **needs error description corrections**\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-17T03:53:29.2522687Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T03:51:51.503262034Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58258,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T03:53:29.2561800Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T03:51:51.665713614Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58258,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document chat isolation, observability, and updated error behaviors in Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 (commit a43d15f) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added several significant features and behavior changes to the AgentServer Responses library:\\n\\n1. **Chat isolation key enforcement** — A new security feature using the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header. When a response is created with this key, all subsequent GET/Cancel/DELETE/InputItems calls must supply the same key; mismatches return 404 to prevent cross-chat data leakage.\\n2. **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`** — A new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs all outbound Foundry storage API calls with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n3. **HttpClient instrumentation** — `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()` is now registered for outbound trace correlation in the OTLP path, exporting outbound HTTP client spans for full distributed traces through the Foundry storage layer.\\n4. **Updated error shapes** — Error responses now align with the container specification: cancel-after-terminal returns `invalid_request_error`, delete-not-found returns 404 (was 400), cancel-after-delete returns 404 (was 400).\\n5. **Metadata constraint validation** — Metadata maps are now validated: ≤16 key-value pairs, keys ≤64 characters, values ≤512 characters.\\n6. **Response ID validation** — Malformed response IDs in the path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are rejected with 400.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key is entirely undocumented** in the README. There is no Key Concepts section or example explaining the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, when to use it, how it enforces isolation across all endpoints, and what error callers receive on key mismatch. This is a security-relevant tenant-isolation feature that hosting developers need to understand.\\n- **Logging/Observability section is stale** — The README Logging section only mentions \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\u0026#34; It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or that outbound HttpClient instrumentation is now registered automatically. Developers troubleshooting Foundry connectivity issues won\\u0026#39;t know these logs exist or how to enable/read them.\\n- **Troubleshooting section has outdated cancel-error description** — The entry \\u0026#34;**400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state.\\u0026#34; is stale. Cancel-after-terminal now returns `invalid_request_error` (still 400 but with a specific error shape), and cancel-after-delete now returns 404. The troubleshooting section does not reflect these corrections.\\n- **Metadata constraints not documented** — The README does not mention that `metadata` maps have enforced limits (16 keys, 64-char keys, 512-char values). Developers who exceed these limits will see unexpected 400 errors with no guidance.\\n- **Response ID format requirements not documented** — No mention that malformed `previous_response_id` or path-parameter response IDs are rejected with 400.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n**1a. Add a \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection to Key Concepts** (insert after the \\u0026#34;Thread safety\\u0026#34; subsection, before `## Examples`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nTo prevent cross-chat data leakage in multi-tenant deployments, the server supports an optional\\n`x-agent-chat-isolation-key` request header. When a response is created with this header, every\\nsubsequent API call against that response (GET, Cancel, DELETE, InputItems) must supply the same\\nkey. Requests with a mismatched or absent key receive a standard **404 Not Found** response,\\nindistinguishable from a response that does not exist.\\n\\nIsolation is enforced both in-flight (before the response is persisted) and for stored responses.\\nNo enforcement is applied when the response was created without an isolation key.\\n\\nThe key is available to your handler via `context.Isolation` for any further application-level\\nisolation logic.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand the Logging subsection** (replace the current single-line paragraph under `### Logging` with):\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nOutbound HTTP calls to the Foundry storage layer are captured by `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`,\\nwhich logs every request and response at **Information** level (success) or **Warning/Error** level\\n(HTTP errors / exceptions), including:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and duration\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`\\n\\nWhen using the OTLP exporter path (`UseOtlpExporter` / non-Azure Monitor), outbound `HttpClient`\\nspans are also exported automatically via `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()`, enabling full\\ndistributed traces through the Foundry storage layer.\\n\\nConfigure minimum log levels in `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Debug\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Update the Troubleshooting \\u0026#34;Common errors\\u0026#34; section** (replace the current bulleted list):\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, `input` items are well-formed, metadata maps contain ≤16\\n  entries with keys ≤64 characters and values ≤512 characters, and that `previous_response_id`\\n  (when provided) is a valid response ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The path-parameter response ID or `previous_response_id`\\n  body field does not match the expected format. Use a valid response ID returned by the server.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`,\\n  `failed`, or `cancelled`). Check the current response status before cancelling.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL,\\n  has been deleted, or was accessed with a mismatched `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background\\n  response is still in-flight. Cancel-after-delete also returns 404.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed code snippets are required for these changes — the additions are conceptual\\nexplanations and updated bullet lists, not code examples. If a sample illustrating chat isolation\\nkey usage is desired, create a new sample file:\\n\\n**New file (optional):** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleChatIsolation.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Sample: Using chat isolation key for multi-tenant deployments\\n// This sample shows how to read the isolation key from ResponseContext.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README already contains all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs Chat isolation subsection added**\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **needs error description corrections**\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
